title: Tibouchina mollis

intro: Tibouchina mollis, also known as the 'Glory Tree,' is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the Melastomataceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can aid in relieving symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Tibouchina mollis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues.
